data.title: Nordic Stocks Tipped to Open Slightly Lower — Market Talk
data.description: Nordic markets are seen opening slightly lower with IG calling the OMXS30 down 0.1% at around 3011. President Trump has threatened 100% tariffs on Canada if the country enters a trade agreement with China. "At the same time, the Democrats are threatening to shutdown parts of the government again if Trump does not agree to withdraw funding for the immigration police," SEB's Sweden chief strategist Olle Holmgren writes. The S&P 500 closed largely unchanged on Friday and stock markets in Asia are mixed, with the Nikkei hit by a clear appreciation of the yen following rumors of currency intervention, Holmgren adds. Stock market futures in both Europe and the U.S. are down a couple of tenths. OMXS30 closed at 3013.63, OMXN40 at 2714.19, and OBX at 1645.20. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: Fnac Darty's preliminary 2025 revenue misses estimates on France underperformance
data.description:
Overview

  • French retailer's preliminary 2025 revenue stable, missing analyst expectations

  • Current operating income for 2025 expected to rise slightly to €203 mln

  • EP Group announced a tender offer for Fnac Darty shares

Outlook

  • Fnac Darty confirms 2030 objectives despite current market challenges

  • Company sees stable revenue at €10,330 mln for 2025

  • Fnac Darty expects operating margin to reach 2.0% by end of 2025

Result Drivers

  • OMNICHANNEL STRATEGY - Co outperformed market despite weak Q4 in France, attributed to its omnichannel and service-oriented strategy

  • FRANCE UNDERPERFORMANCE - Q4 activity in France declined, impacting overall revenue growth

  • EUROPEAN GROWTH - Rest of Europe recorded satisfactory performance, with Italy contributing to slight increase in operating income

Key Details

Metric

FY Revenue

Beat/Miss

Miss

Actual

EUR 10.33 bln

Consensus Estimate

EUR 10.56 bln (6 Analysts)

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 4 "strong buy" or "buy", 2 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the computer & electronics retailers peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Fnac Darty SA is €38.00, about 25.6% above its January 23 closing price of €30.25

  • The stock recently traded at 12 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 11 three months ago

data.title: Heating Oil Rises to Over 2-Month High
data.description:
US heating oil futures rose more than 3% to around $2.51 per gallon, reaching an over two-month high amid increased demand for heating fuel due to extreme winter storm.

Strong energy consumption has provided firm support to distillate prices at a time when inventories remain tight and refinery runs constrained, eroding the usual seasonal buffer and tightening near-term product availability.

Meanwhile, crude benchmarks stayed well supported by ongoing geopolitical risks and supply concerns, reinforcing feedstock strength for refined products.

Additional support came from a softer US dollar, which makes greenback-priced commodities cheaper for buyers holding other currencies.

data.title: Palladium Climbs to Three-Year High
data.description:
Palladium futures rose above $2,000 per ounce, the highest in over three years, as supply concerns intensified amid heightened geopolitical risks.

Reports over the weekend that Canada could face 100% tariffs if it proceeds with a trade deal with China raised fears of potential disruptions to North American metal flows, given Canada’s status as a major global producer of palladium.

Supporting prices further, UBS recently raised its palladium forecast by $300 per ounce to $1,800, citing strong investment inflows.

Demand was also bolstered by China, where the launch of yuan-denominated platinum futures in Guangzhou boosted trading activity across the platinum group metals.

Year-to-date, palladium has gained 25.78%, and although industrial use in automotive catalytic converters has softened due to the rise of electric vehicles, tight supply and continued investor interest suggest further gains in the near term.

data.title: BioArctic's Partner Eisai Receives Priority Review For Leqembi Iqlik Subcutaneous Autoinjector
data.description:
TOKYO (dpa-AFX) - BioArctic AB (BRCTF) announced on Monday that its partner Eisai (ESALY) has received Priority Review from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for the supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for the Leqembi Iqlik subcutaneous autoinjector.

The Prescription Drug User Fee Act (PDUFA) action date is set for May 24, 2026.

Leqembi is currently approved in more than 50 countries and regions for the treatment of early Alzheimer's disease.

If approved, the Leqembi Iqlik subcutaneous (SC) autoinjector could provide an alternative to the current intravenous (IV) dosing method.

The sBLA is supported by positive data from the Phase 3 Clarity Alzheimer's disease open-label extension (OLE) study, which evaluated SC administration of lecanemab across various doses.

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Ryanair Q3 Profit Declines; Revenue Up 9%
data.description:
BRUSSELS/FRANKFURT/PARIS (dpa-AFX) - Ryanair Holdings plc (RY4C.DE, RYA.L, RYA.IR) reported that its third quarter profit all attributable to equity holders of parent declined to 30.4 million euros from 148.6 million euros, prior year. IFRS earnings per ordinary share was 0.0286 euros compared to 0.1360 euros. Profit - before exceptional charge was 115.4 million euros compared to 148.6 million euros.

Third quarter total operating revenues increased to 3.21 billion euros from 2.96 billion euros, last year. Scheduled revenue increased 10% to 2.10 billion euros. Traffic grew 6% to 47.5 million.

Ryanair now expects fiscal 2026 traffic to grow 4% to almost 208 million passengers. The company said it is cautiously guiding fiscal 2026 profit after tax, pre-exceptional, in a range of 2.13 billion to 2.23 billion euros.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Ryanair Upgrades Fiscal 2026 Outlook on Strong Demand
data.description:
(06:01 GMT) *Ryanair 3Q Rev EUR3.21B
(06:01 GMT) *Analysts Saw Ryanair 3Q Rev EUR3.17B
(06:01 GMT) *Ryanair 3Q Rev Grew 9%
(06:02 GMT) *Ryanair Flew 6% More Passengers in 3Q
(06:02 GMT) *Ryanair 3Q Load Factor Flat at 92%
(06:03 GMT) *Ryanair 3Q Scheduled Rev Grew 10% to EUR2.10B
(06:03 GMT) *Ryanair 3Q Ancillary Rev Grew 7% to EUR1.11B
(06:04 GMT) *Ryanair 3Q Operating Costs Rose 6% to EUR3.11B
(06:05 GMT) *Ryanair Expects FY26 Traffic to Rise 4% to Almost 208M Passengers
(06:05 GMT) *Ryanair Had Previously Expected FY26 Traffic to Reach 207M Passengers
(06:06 GMT) *Ryanair: Fares Are Trending Ahead of Prior Year
(06:06 GMT) *Ryanair: Full-Year Fares Will Exceed the 7% Growth Previously Guided by 1%-2%
(06:07 GMT) *Ryanair Expects FY26 Fares in the Range of EUR2.13B-EUR2.23B
(06:09 GMT) *Ryanair 3Q Net Pft EUR30.4M
(06:09 GMT) *Analysts Saw Ryanair 3Q Net Profit EUR69M
(06:10 GMT) *Ryanair 3Q Average Fares Rose 4%
(06:33 GMT) Ryanair Upgrades Fiscal 2026 Outlook on Strong Demand

By Cristina Gallardo

Ryanair upgraded its fiscal 2026 guidance due to strong demand and earlier-than-expected Boeing aircraft deliveries.

The Irish low-cost carrier on Monday said it made revenue of 3.21 billion euros ($3.81 billion) for its quarter ended Dec. 31, up 9% compared with the same period of the previous year.

Of this, 1.11 billion euros was ancillary revenue, which covers services such as baggage fees and food on board.

The airline flew 47.5 million passengers in its third quarter, up 6% on year.

Fares increased 4% on average while load factor, a measure of how full its planes are, stood flat at 92% for the quarter, Ryanair said.

The airline said it now expects fiscal 2026 traffic to grow by 4% to almost 208 million passengers, surpassing a previous forecast of 207 million passengers.

It also said fares are trending ahead of the previous year, with full-year fares now expected to exceed the previously-guided 7% growth.

Write to Cristina Gallardo at cristina.gallardo@wsj.com

data.title: European Stocks Set for Lower Open
data.description:
European equity markets were poised to open lower on Monday as risk aversion spread across global markets amid heightened geopolitical and trade uncertainty.

In the latest developments, US President Donald Trump warned that Canada could face 100% tariffs if it were to strike a trade deal with China, although Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said Ottawa has no intention of pursuing a free trade agreement with Beijing.

Trump also cautioned that a US aircraft carrier strike group is heading toward the Middle East as tensions with Iran intensify.

In Europe, investors will turn their attention to Germany’s Ifo Business Climate survey for January and Spain’s producer price data for December, with no major corporate earnings scheduled.

In premarket trading, Euro Stoxx 50 and Stoxx 600 futures were each down about 0.1%.

data.title: CBOT soybeans may rise into $10.78-1/2 to $10.80-1/2 range
data.description:
Key points:
  • For more technical analyses, please clickTECH/C

The CBOT soybean March contract (SH26) may break resistance at $10.75 per bushel and rise into the $10.78-1/2 to $10.80-1/2 range.

A rising channel suggests a target around $10.80-1/2. A projection analysis on the uptrend from $10.52 indicates a higher target zone of $10.83-3/4 to $10.89-1/4.

Support is at $10.71-3/4, a break below which could trigger a fall into the $10.66-1/4 to $10.69-1/2 range. On the daily chart, the contract has broken a resistance at $10.69-3/4.

The break could lead to a gain into the $10.89-3/4 to $11.05-3/4 range.

* Wang Tao is a Reuters market analyst for commodities and energy technicals. Views expressed are his own.

** No information in this analysis should be considered as being business, financial, or legal advice. Each reader should consult their own professional or other advisers for business, financial, or legal advice regarding the products mentioned in the analyses.

data.title: ZAWYA-SNG: Saudi: Miral Dental’s board greenlights dividends for H1-25
data.description:
Staff Writer

Riyadh - The board of Miral Dental Clinics Company has approved cash dividends of SAR 4 million, equivalent to 20% of the company’s SAR 20 million capital, for the first half (H1) of 2025.

The Tadawul-listed company will distribute a dividend of SAR 2 per share for 2 million eligible shares, according to a bourse statement.

Meanwhile, the eligibility and distribution dates will be on 2 and 11 February 2026, respectively.
